fields.brief_block: If there is some competent evidence, even though contradicted, to support each fact necessary to make out the State's case, the jury's verdict will be upheld. Santiago v. State, 314 Ga. App. 623, 623, 724 S.E.2d 793, 794 (2012).
fields.opinion_block: As long as there is some competent evidence, even though contradicted, to support each fact necessary to make out the state's case, the jury's verdict will be upheld.
fields.finding_analysis: Santiago v. State recites this exact standard of appellate review for sufficiency of the evidence in a quoted passage near the opening of the opinion, and the brief's sentence tracks that language almost verbatim. The proposition is directly and accurately supported.
